channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government and TNBC work together to improve business conditions in Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government, through the Tanzania National Business Council (TNBC), is making strong efforts to tackle challenges facing the country's business environment. During the third session of TN...

item.title: Iran and Israel escalate conflict amid Beirut strikes and threats of retaliation
item.description: The Middle East conflict has intensified following the assassination of Iranian security chief Ali Larijani in an Israeli airstrike. Iran’s military has vowed decisive retaliation, also confirming the...

item.title: US military redeployment to Iran raises security concerns in the Pacific
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East escalates, the United States has started moving military resources westward, which is raising alarm among security planners in Asia. Elements of an anti-missile batt...

item.title: Sri Lanka Sets Wednesday Holiday to Save Fuel Amid Economic and Energy Challenges
item.description: Sri Lanka don announce say every Wednesday go dey public holiday as government dey try reduce fuel wahala wey don deep. Di main reason na because di country dey face serious economic and energy crisis...

item.title: Pakistan-China Trade via Khunjerab Pass Shows Significant Growth
item.description: Trade activity between Pakistan and China through the Khunjerab Pass has picked up strongly this year, showing a notable increase in both exports and imports. Up till March 2026, 1,774 consignments fr...

item.title: Birdwatching gains popularity among younger generations
item.description: Birdwatching, once thought to be a pastime mainly enjoyed by older men in cargo shorts, is experiencing a resurgence among younger Australians. Mitch Kenny, a 28-year-old Penrith Panthers hooker, has ...

item.title: Benefits and drawbacks of couples sleeping in separate beds for health and relationships
item.description: Sharing a bed with a partner is often viewed as a key aspect of romantic relationships, with around 82% of couples in the US doing so. Historically, sleeping together was common, but concerns over hyg...

item.title: UK bans AI video app ad that implied removing women's clothing
item.description: The UK Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) has banned an advert for the AI video and image editing app, PixVideo, which implied that users could digitally remove a woman's clothing. The YouTube adve...

item.title: Daily Horoscopes for March 18, 2026 by Sally Brompton
item.description: This daily horoscope column by Sally Brompton, published in The Globe and Mail, provides astrological guidance for all zodiac signs on March 18, 2026. The day features a Pisces new moon, which emphasi...

item.title: MaWhoo’s Journey from Humble Beginnings to Metro FM Music Award Nominee
item.description: Local singer MaWhoo has rapidly become one of South Africa’s standout music talents, going from performing small gigs in 2022 to securing seven nominations for the 2026 Metro FM Music Awards. At 31, s...

item.title: Shaun Ryder Reflects on Music, Addiction, and Life in Happy Mondays
item.description: Shaun Ryder, the frontman of Happy Mondays, reflects on his tumultuous life and career in his new memoir, '24 Hour Party Person'. Known for his chaotic youth, Ryder discusses growing up with ADHD, his...

item.title: IUML Candidate from Kondotty Confident as Kerala Assembly Elections Approach
item.description: Ahead of the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, TP Ashraf Ali, the Indian Union Muslim League (IUML) candidate from Kondotty constituency, has stated that the party is fully prepared for the polls. Speak...

item.title: Pakistan Offers Mediation to Reduce Middle East Tensions
item.description: Information Minister Attaullah Tarar has confirmed that Pakistan is prepared to play a diplomatic role to help reduce tensions in the Middle East as the conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran worse...

item.title: Macau Woman Briefly Hospitalised After Being Frightened by Humanoid Robot
item.description: A woman in Macau had to be taken to hospital after being startled by a humanoid robot while she was on an evening walk and using her mobile phone. According to police, she did not suffer any physical ...

item.title: Morocco Awarded AFCON 2025 Title After Senegal Forfeit
item.description: Moroccan football fans went wild on the streets after the Confederation of African Football (CAF) Appeals Board officially awarded Morocco the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) title. The drama start...

item.title: Analysis of Nigel Farage's Paid Cameo Messages Reveals Controversial Endorsements
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, has earned over £374,000 through Cameo, a platform where celebrities record personalised video messages. An investigation into more than 4,000 of these videos reveal...

item.title: Sporting CP overturns three-goal deficit to reach Champions League quarter-finals
item.description: Sporting CP make history as dem overcome Bodo/Glimt for UEFA Champions League quarter-finals. For first leg, Bodo/Glimt shock Sporting with 3–0 win, showing say Norwegian side fit perform well even fo...

item.title: Microsoft restructures Copilot teams to accelerate AI and superintelligence development
item.description: Microsoft has recently announced a major reorganisation of its Copilot teams, aiming to unify both commercial and consumer versions of its AI assistant. This step comes as a response to rising competi...

item.title: Two Arrested Over R800,000 Kickbacks Linked to Sedfa in Limpopo
item.description: In Tzaneen, Limpopo, two individuals—a company director and a financial adviser—have been arrested in connection with an alleged corruption and fraud scheme involving over R800,000 in kickbacks from t...

item.title: Sofronio Vasquez releases updated rendition of 'Tadhana' for 'Secrets of Hotel 88' soundtrack
item.description: Filipino singer Sofronio Vasquez has recently recorded a new version of the popular song 'Tadhana' as part of the official soundtrack for the upcoming television series 'Secrets of Hotel 88'. Known fo...
